metadata.mission: Add CI sandboxing support with a human review gate, allowlist layering, and ephemeral cast ci run lifecycle that reuses existing credential isolation infrastructure.
assumptions: TOCTOU between gate check and execution is a known gap (not SHA-pinned in v1). Window is small (seconds). Documented in threat model.
Self-approval enforcement is delegated to GitHub branch protection rules, not enforced in ci_gate.py
CI containers use regular auth mode (not bot mode), so SANDBOX_BRANCH_PATTERN changes only affect bot-mode scenarios
GCS write scope is bounded by IAM permissions of the injected credential, not by allowlist paths
Fork PRs are not supported by the review gate (same-repo branches only)

metadata.description: Add GCS endpoints to the default allowlist and implement the allowlist merge mechanism that CI and custom deployments will use.

task-1-1.title: Add GCS read-only HTTP endpoints to default allowlist

metadata.description: Add storage.googleapis.com HTTP endpoint to http_endpoints section with GET, HEAD methods and paths /storage/v1/** and /download/storage/v1/**. No domains change needed — *.googleapis.com is already in the DNS allowlist (line 30).
acceptance_criteria: storage.googleapis.com entry exists in http_endpoints with methods [GET, HEAD]

metadata.description: Add merge support to load_allowlist_config() (line 499): after loading the primary allowlist, check PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var. If set, load the extra YAML and validate its schema — same per-entry field validation as the primary loader (host, methods, paths required on each http_endpoint entry). Only the top-level 'at least one domain/endpoint required' check is relaxed. Merge: domains via ordered dedup (preserve first-seen order, base then extra), http_endpoints pre-merged by host (for same host, union methods and paths into a single entry so that the policy engine's first-host-match behavior sees all allowed methods/paths), blocked_paths appended. IMPORTANT: The policy engine (policy_engine.py:455) may stop at the first matching host entry — so same-host entries from base and extra MUST be combined into one entry, not just appended.
acceptance_criteria: Domains merged via ordered dedup (preserve first-seen order)
Same-host http_endpoints are pre-merged: methods unioned, paths unioned into a single entry
Empty domains/endpoints in extra file is valid (no error)

metadata.description: Create config/allowlist-ci-extra.yaml with GCS write methods (PUT, POST, DELETE) for paths /storage/v1/** and /upload/storage/v1/**. Version 1.0, empty domains list. This is the CI-only additive layer beyond the base allowlist.
acceptance_criteria: File contains storage.googleapis.com with methods [PUT, POST, DELETE]

metadata.description: Validate allowlist YAML files parse correctly and merge logic works. Run python -c to validate YAML syntax for both files. Run unit tests for merge logic.
Extract reusable functions from destroy.py and docker.py that the CI runner will call, without changing existing behavior.

Extract lines 55–196 into destroy_impl(name, keep_worktree=False, force=False, skip_tmux=False, skip_branch_cleanup=False). Existing destroy() Click command becomes a thin wrapper: validate name, then call destroy_impl(). Inside destroy_impl(): confirmation prompt guarded by force (and existing SANDBOX_NONINTERACTIVE); step 1 (tmux kill) guarded by skip_tmux; step 10 (branch cleanup) guarded by skip_branch_cleanup; all other steps unconditional. CI calls: destroy_impl(name, force=True, skip_tmux=True, skip_branch_cleanup=True).
acceptance_criteria: destroy_impl() function exists with 5 params (name, keep_worktree, force, skip_tmux, skip_branch_cleanup)
Existing cast destroy behavior is unchanged (thin wrapper calls destroy_impl)

task-2-2.title: Add compose_extras param to get_compose_command() and compose_up()

metadata.description: Add compose_extras: list[str] | None = None param to get_compose_command() (line 237). When non-empty, append -f <script_dir>/<path> for each entry to the compose file chain. Thread compose_extras through compose_up() (line 310) and compose_down() (line 446). This replaces a ci_mode boolean with a general mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: compose_up() accepts and threads compose_extras to get_compose_command()
compose_down() accepts and threads compose_extras to get_compose_command()
When compose_extras is empty/None, behavior unchanged
When compose_extras=['docker-compose.ci.yml'], the -f flag is appended

metadata.description: New function exec_in_container_streaming(container_id, *args, timeout=3600) -> int. Uses subprocess.Popen with stdout=None, stderr=None (inherits parent FDs for real-time streaming). Calls proc.wait(timeout=timeout). On TimeoutExpired: proc.kill() + proc.wait() to reap, then docker stop <container_id> --time 5 with 15s subprocess timeout as backstop. Returns exit code or raises TimeoutError. Separate from existing exec_in_container() which keeps its CompletedProcess return type.
acceptance_criteria: Uses Popen with inherited stdout/stderr (not captured)
Returns integer exit code on success
On timeout: calls proc.kill(), proc.wait(), then docker stop as backstop
shell=False used for all subprocess calls

metadata.description: cast destroy <sandbox> behavior unchanged. Unit tests for destroy_impl skip flags and streaming exec success/failure/timeout.
Implement the human review gate (security-critical) with tightened effective-review-state semantics, and agent branch naming convention as standalone modules.

task-3-1.title: Create ci_gate.py with review gate logic

metadata.description:
Create foundry_sandbox/ci_gate.py with check_review_gate(repo, branch, gh_token) -> bool. Define GATE_EXEMPT_BRANCHES = frozenset({'main', 'master', 'develop', 'production'}) directly (no cross-package import from branch_types.py). Gate logic:
1. If branch in GATE_EXEMPT_BRANCHES → return True (log as exempt)
2. Call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?head={owner}:{branch}&state=open
3. If no PR exists → return False (fail closed, log as blocked)
4. For each open PR, fetch reviews and compute effective review state per reviewer: only the most recent review per reviewer counts; dismissed reviews are discarded; CHANGES_REQUESTED supersedes earlier APPROVED from same reviewer
5. If any reviewer has effective state CHANGES_REQUESTED → PR is blocked regardless of other approvals
6. Gate passes only if at least one reviewer has effective APPROVED and no reviewer has effective CHANGES_REQUESTED
7. Short-circuit on first passing PR if multiple open PRs exist

Self-approval enforcement delegated to GitHub branch protection (documented). Fork PRs not supported (same-repo only, head uses {owner}:{branch}). Network/auth errors raise exceptions (never silently pass). Gate decisions logged via log_info with branch, repo, result, and reason.
acceptance_criteria: check_review_gate(repo, branch, gh_token) -> bool function exists
GATE_EXEMPT_BRANCHES is frozenset with main, master, develop, production
Exempt branches return True immediately with logging
No PR for branch returns False (fail closed)
Effective review state computed per reviewer (most recent wins)
Dismissed reviews discarded from effective state calculation
CHANGES_REQUESTED from any reviewer blocks even if another reviewer APPROVED
API errors (401, 403, network) raise exceptions, never silently pass
Gate decisions logged with branch, repo, result, reason
Uses requests or urllib for HTTP calls, not subprocess gh

metadata.description: Add prefix: str | None = None param to _generate_branch_name() (line 144). When provided, prepend <prefix>/ to the branch name. Remove unused from_branch parameter. Validate prefix against safe git-ref rules (reject .., @{, .lock, leading/trailing dots). Update all callers.
acceptance_criteria: Prefix validated against git-ref safety rules

metadata.description: Update SANDBOX_BRANCH_PATTERN (line 66) from r'^refs/heads/sandbox/' to r'^refs/heads/(sandbox|agent)/' to allow agent/* branches in bot mode.
acceptance_criteria: Pattern does NOT match other prefixes

metadata.description: Unit tests for all gate logic including effective review state, CHANGES_REQUESTED blocking, dismissed reviews, API errors. Branch prefix validation tests.
Wire everything together into the cast ci run command with ephemeral setup/exec/teardown lifecycle.

task-4-1.title: Create ci_runner.py with CiRunConfig and run_ci_job()

metadata.description:
Create foundry_sandbox/ci_runner.py with:
- CiRunConfig dataclass: repo, branch, command (list[str]), timeout (int, default 3600), name (str | None), keep_on_failure (bool, default False). No skip_review_gate field.
- run_ci_job(config: CiRunConfig) -> int orchestrator:
  1. Validate: parse args, check Docker available, validate GITHUB_TOKEN present
  2. Review gate: check_review_gate(repo, branch, gh_token). Fail fast if blocked.
  3. Setup: call _new_setup() with isolate_credentials=True, allow_pr=False, compose_extras=['docker-compose.ci.yml'], ttl_seconds=config.timeout + 300. Thread ttl_seconds through _new_setup() -> setup_proxy_registration() -> proxy_register().
  4. Execute: exec_in_container_streaming() with timeout
  5. Teardown: destroy_impl(name, force=True, skip_tmux=True, skip_branch_cleanup=True) in try/finally. If keep_on_failure and command failed, skip teardown.
  6. Return command exit code

TTL plumbing: setup_proxy_registration() (proxy.py:326) currently calls proxy_register() with default ttl_seconds=0. Add ttl_seconds param to setup_proxy_registration() and thread through from _new_setup(). CI passes timeout+300; non-CI callers continue to use default 0 (no expiry).

Lifecycle events logged to stderr via log_info/log_warn. On setup failure, call _rollback_new()."
acceptance_criteria: CiRunConfig dataclass with all fields, no skip_review_gate
Proxy registration uses ttl_seconds=timeout+300 threaded through _new_setup -> setup_proxy_registration -> proxy_register
setup_proxy_registration() accepts ttl_seconds param (default 0 for non-CI)
Teardown always runs via try/finally (unless keep_on_failure)
keep_on_failure with failed command skips teardown

metadata.description: Add compose_extras: list[str] | None = None and ttl_seconds: int = 0 as keyword-only params to _new_setup() (after anthropic_base_url). Thread compose_extras to compose_up() call. Thread ttl_seconds through to setup_proxy_registration(). Use None default (not mutable []) and normalize internally. Default None/0 preserves existing behavior for cast new. CI passes compose_extras=['docker-compose.ci.yml'] and ttl_seconds=timeout+300."
acceptance_criteria: _new_setup() accepts compose_extras: list[str] | None = None param
Default None/0 preserves existing cast new behavior
No mutable default (uses None, normalized internally)

metadata.description: Create docker-compose.ci.yml as third overlay in compose stack. unified-proxy service: mount config/allowlist-ci-extra.yaml to /etc/unified-proxy/allowlist-ci-extra.yaml:ro, set PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var. dev service: stdin_open: false, tty: false. No restart policy override needed (Docker Compose defaults to 'no').
acceptance_criteria: PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH env var set on unified-proxy
dev service has stdin_open: false and tty: false
Stacks correctly with base + credential-isolation compose files
docker compose config validates with all three files

metadata.description: Create foundry_sandbox/commands/ci.py with Click command group and run subcommand. Arguments: repo (required), branch (required), command (variadic, after --). Options: --timeout INT (default 3600), --keep-on-failure flag, --name TEXT. No --skip-review-gate flag. Reads GITHUB_TOKEN from environment. Calls run_ci_job() and sys.exit() with returned code.
acceptance_criteria: ci Click group with run subcommand
repo and branch as required arguments

metadata.description: Add 'ci' entry to _LAZY_COMMANDS dict (line 37): 'ci': ('foundry_sandbox.commands.ci', 'ci')

docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.credential-isolation.yml -f docker-compose.ci.yml config validates. Unit tests for run_ci_job lifecycle, failure propagation, timeout, setup failure, keep-on-failure, proxy TTL.
Document CI usage, threat model, and provide reference templates for GitHub Actions and allowlist customization.

Create docs/usage/ci.md documenting: cast ci run usage and flags, CI network profile and allowlist customization (base + PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH merge + custom extra YAML), agent branch prefix convention (advisory, not gate-linked), human review gate behavior (all non-exempt branches gated, fail-closed, effective review state per reviewer, CHANGES_REQUESTED blocking, no skip mechanism), exempt branches (main/master/develop/production exact names only with rationale for no prefix exemptions), GitHub Actions integration template, secret injection model (credential isolation, proxy holds real creds, CI sees placeholders), proxy TTL for CI registrations, debugging failed CI runs (--keep-on-failure, cast attach, stderr lifecycle logs), agent branch lifecycle (not auto-cleaned).
acceptance_criteria: All cast ci run flags documented
Review gate behavior fully described including effective state semantics

task-5-2.title: Add CI Threat Model section to sandbox-threats.md

metadata.description: Add CI Threat Model section covering: untrusted agent code in CI, review gate defense (all branches gated, exact names exempt, no prefix exemptions, no skip), why prefix exemptions excluded (branch isolation allows sandbox pushes to release/*/hotfix/*), TOCTOU gap as known limitation (documented, not SHA-pinned in v1), effective review state semantics, CI network profile rationale, GCS write scope bounded by IAM not allowlist, proxy TTL prevents stale registrations, timeout handling gap (proc.kill kills docker exec not container process, docker stop backstop, TTL bounds exposure), orphan containers (resource consumption, name collision, proxy TTL as credential-only mitigation), GitHub API rate limits (fail closed), fork PRs not supported, scope exclusions (resource limits, orphan cleanup automation, webapp/Jenkins CI deferred).
acceptance_criteria: CI Threat Model section exists in sandbox-threats.md
All known gaps documented (TOCTOU, proc.kill gap, orphan containers, rate limits, fork PRs)

metadata.description: Create docs/ci/github-action-template.yml with a documented PR-triggered workflow template showing cast ci run usage with ${{ github.repository }} and ${{ github.head_ref }}. Include ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from secrets. No FOUNDRY_CI_ALLOW_SKIP_GATE env var.
acceptance_criteria: Uses cast ci run with github.repository and github.head_ref
Injects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from secrets

metadata.description: Create docs/ci/allowlist-extra-example.yaml as a reference example for org-specific CI allowlist customization. Include example entries for Cypress Cloud (*.cypress.io) and Turborepo remote cache (*.turbo.build) with comments explaining usage.
acceptance_criteria: Example includes Cypress Cloud and Turborepo entries
Comments explain how to use with PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH

metadata.description: All docs render correctly, reference correct flags/behavior, and threat model covers all known gaps.
Comprehensive unit test coverage for all new modules and integration tests for compose stack and allowlist merge.

Create tests/unit/test_allowlist_merge.py with 13 test cases: no PROXY_ALLOWLIST_EXTRA_PATH set (base unchanged), extra with additional domains (ordered dedup, no duplicates), extra with additional http_endpoints for new host (appended), extra with additional blocked_paths (appended), extra with empty domains/endpoints (no error), extra path does not exist (ConfigError), extra has invalid YAML (ConfigError), extra entry missing required methods field (ConfigError), extra entry missing required host field (ConfigError), extra entry missing required paths field (ConfigError), extra entry with methods not a list (ConfigError), same-host entries pre-merged (methods unioned, paths unioned into single entry), domain order preserved (base entries first, then extra)."
acceptance_criteria: 13 test cases covering all merge scenarios
Ordered dedup verified for domains (first-seen order preserved)
Same-host http_endpoints pre-merged (methods and paths unioned)

metadata.description: Create tests/unit/test_ci_gate.py with 22+ test cases: exempt branches (main, master, develop, production return True), non-exempt prefix branches (release/v1.0 and hotfix/urgent without PR return False), release/v1.0 with approved PR passes, hotfix/urgent with approved PR passes, feature/foo no PR (False), feature/foo PR with no reviews (False), feature/foo PR with CHANGES_REQUESTED only (False), feature/foo PR with APPROVED (True), effective state per reviewer (most recent wins), CHANGES_REQUESTED blocks even with another reviewer APPROVED, dismissed reviews discarded, reviewer APPROVED then CHANGES_REQUESTED = blocked, reviewer CHANGES_REQUESTED then APPROVED = passes, agent/fix with approved PR passes, agent/fix no PR (False), multiple PRs short-circuit on first approved, empty reviews list (False), API 401 raises, API 403 raises, network error raises, non-2xx reviews API raises, gate decisions logged with branch/repo/result/reason. Use unittest.mock to mock HTTP calls.
acceptance_criteria: 22+ test cases covering all gate scenarios
Effective review state per reviewer tested (most recent wins)
All error conditions raise (never silently pass)

metadata.description: Create tests/unit/test_ci_runner.py with 7 test cases: successful job lifecycle (setup, exec, teardown, exit 0), command fails exit 1 (teardown still runs, exit 1 propagated), command times out (process killed, docker stop called, teardown runs, TimeoutError), setup failure (_rollback_new called, no exec attempted), keep_on_failure with failed command (sandbox left running), Docker not running (clear error before setup), proxy registered with TTL = timeout + 300. Use unittest.mock to mock _new_setup, destroy_impl, exec_in_container_streaming, check_review_gate.

Create tests/unit/test_destroy_impl.py with 4 test cases: skip_tmux=True (step 1 not called), skip_branch_cleanup=True (step 10 not called), both skip flags False (full sequence), force=True (confirmation prompt skipped). Use unittest.mock to mock subprocess calls and click.confirm.
acceptance_criteria: 4 test cases for destroy_impl skip flags

metadata.description: Create tests/unit/test_exec_streaming.py with 4 test cases: existing exec_in_container returns CompletedProcess (unchanged), exec_in_container_streaming success returns exit code 0, exec_in_container_streaming failure returns non-zero, exec_in_container_streaming timeout (proc.kill called, docker stop called with 15s timeout, TimeoutError raised).
acceptance_criteria: Timeout handling with kill + docker stop verified

metadata.description: pytest tests/unit/test_allowlist_merge.py tests/unit/test_ci_gate.py tests/unit/test_ci_runner.py tests/unit/test_destroy_impl.py tests/unit/test_exec_streaming.py -v — all pass. ./scripts/ci-local.sh — no regressions.
